description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly JS API caused by `DefaultReferenceValue()` in `src/wasm/wasm-js.cc` returning a JS `null` value instead of `wasm_null` for the `kExn` and `kNoExn` heap types. Commit 9997fc0 internalized the exn type hierarchy to use `wasm_null` as the canonical null sentinel for these types, but `DefaultReferenceValue()` was not updated accordingly and still returns the JS null value (`isolate->factory()->null_value()`). This contradicts `ValueTypeBase::use_wasm_null()`, which correctly returns `true` for `kExn`/`kNoExn`. The resulting inconsistency means a `ref null noexn` typed slot can hold a JS null value—an object that does not belong to the exn type hierarchy—creating a type confusion. This confusion can be pivoted into arbitrary Wasm type confusion via Turboshaft's typer optimizations (through `RefineTypeKnowledge`/`BrOnCastFailImpl`), and can also be exploited through Liftoff-compiled functions that return an uninhabited `ref noexn` typed value without trapping. Triggering the vulnerability requires the staged flag `--experimental-wasm-exnref`.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6000165 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
